criteria.given: scripts/score-inject.sh and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh after applying the LOCK_TIMEOUT parameterization
criteria.when: bash -n is run on both scripts
criteria.then: both exit 0 with no stderr output

criteria.given: scripts/score-inject.sh and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh after the change
criteria.when: grep is run for the LOCK_TIMEOUT assignment in each file
criteria.then: each file contains exactly one line matching LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" and that line appears before the flock call in the same script

commands.description: score-inject.sh contains exactly one LOCK_TIMEOUT assignment
lifecycle-baseline-import.sh contains exactly one LOCK_TIMEOUT assignment
LOCK_TIMEOUT declaration appears before flock call in score-inject.sh (line number of assignment < line number of flock call)
commands.command: decl=$(grep -n 'LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"' scripts/score-inject.sh | cut -d: -f1); flock=$(grep -n 'flock.*LOCK_TIMEOUT' scripts/score-inject.sh | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); [ -n "$decl" ] && [ -n "$flock" ] && [ "$decl" -lt "$flock" ] && echo OK

commands.description: LOCK_TIMEOUT declaration appears before flock call in lifecycle-baseline-import.sh
commands.command: decl=$(grep -n 'LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"' scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh | cut -d: -f1); flock=$(grep -n 'flock.*LOCK_TIMEOUT' scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); [ -n "$decl" ] && [ -n "$flock" ] && [ "$decl" -lt "$flock" ] && echo OK

criteria.given: scripts/score-inject.sh and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh after the change
criteria.when: grep is run for bare flock integer literals and for the variable-form flock calls
criteria.then: no bare flock -s -w 10 or flock -w 10 remains; score-inject.sh uses flock -s -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 8; lifecycle-baseline-import.sh uses flock -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 9

commands.description: No bare integer 10 remains in flock calls in either target script
score-inject.sh uses the correct parameterized shared flock form
lifecycle-baseline-import.sh uses the correct parameterized exclusive flock form

criteria.given: scripts/score-inject.sh and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh after the change
criteria.when: grep is run for hardcoded '10s' and for the variable form '${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s' in stderr messages
criteria.then: no adjacent stderr message contains the hardcoded string 'within 10s'; both files contain 'within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s'

commands.description: No 'within 10s' string in either target script
